base_annual_fee.source_clause: The Base Annual Fee shall be INR 14,40,00,000 (Rupees Fourteen Crore Forty Lakhs only) per annum, payable monthly at INR 1,20,00,000 (Rupees One Crore Twenty Lakhs only) per month, on or before the 5th working day of each calendar month.

base_monthly_fee.source_clause: The Base Annual Fee shall be INR 14,40,00,000 (Rupees Fourteen Crore Forty Lakhs only) per annum, payable monthly at INR 1,20,00,000 (Rupees One Crore Twenty Lakhs only) per month, on or before the 5th working day of each calendar month.

escalation.source_clause: The Base Annual Fee shall be escalated on April 1 of each Contract Year by the change in the Wholesale Price Index (WPI — All Commodities) for the month of January, as published by the Office of the Economic Adviser, Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India. The escalation shall be subject to a maximum cap of 8% per annum and a floor of 0% per annum (i.e. fees shall not decrease due to WPI deflation).

variable_component.source_clause: In addition to the Base Monthly Fee, the Service Provider shall invoice a Variable O&M Component at the rate of INR 0.04 (Four Paise) per kWh of net energy generated by the Wind Farm, measured at the point of grid connection and certified by the SCADA system. This variable component shall be billed quarterly within 30 days of quarter end.

availability_guarantee_pct.source_clause: The Service Provider guarantees an annual Wind Turbine Availability (WTA) of not less than 96.0% (Ninety Six percent). WTA shall be calculated as: (Total Available Hours − Grid Unavailability Hours − Force Majeure Hours) / (Total Hours in the Period) × 100. Measurement shall be on an annual basis, April 1 to March 31.

ld_rate_per_pp.source_clause: For each full or partial percentage point shortfall below the Guaranteed WTA of 96.0%, the Service Provider shall pay Liquidated Damages (LDs) at the rate of 0.5% (Zero point Five percent) of the Base Annual Fee per percentage point of shortfall. Maximum LD liability shall be capped at 15% of the Base Annual Fee per annum.

ld_cap_pct.source_clause: For each full or partial percentage point shortfall below the Guaranteed WTA of 96.0%, the Service Provider shall pay Liquidated Damages (LDs) at the rate of 0.5% (Zero point Five percent) of the Base Annual Fee per percentage point of shortfall. Maximum LD liability shall be capped at 15% of the Base Annual Fee per annum.

bonus_threshold_pct.source_clause: In the event that the annual Wind Turbine Availability exceeds 98.0% (Ninety Eight percent), the Service Provider shall be entitled to a Performance Bonus at the rate of 1% (One percent) of the Base Annual Fee for each full percentage point above the 98.0% threshold. The maximum Performance Bonus shall not exceed 5% of the Base Annual Fee in any Contract Year.

bonus_rate_per_pp.source_clause: In the event that the annual Wind Turbine Availability exceeds 98.0% (Ninety Eight percent), the Service Provider shall be entitled to a Performance Bonus at the rate of 1% (One percent) of the Base Annual Fee for each full percentage point above the 98.0% threshold. The maximum Performance Bonus shall not exceed 5% of the Base Annual Fee in any Contract Year.

payment_terms_days.source_clause: All invoices submitted by the Service Provider shall be paid within 45 (Forty Five) days from the date of receipt of a valid tax invoice along with requisite supporting documents. In case of dispute, the undisputed portion shall be paid within the stipulated period and the disputed amount shall be resolved as per Clause 11.

late_payment_interest.source_clause: Any amounts not paid within the stipulated 45-day period shall attract simple interest at the rate of the State Bank of India (SBI) Base Rate plus 2% per annum (SBI Base Rate + 2% p.a.) on a daily basis from the due date until the date of actual payment.

renewal_notice_months.source_clause: Either Party wishing to terminate or renegotiate this Agreement at the end of the initial Term or any Extended Term shall provide written notice of no less than 12 (Twelve) calendar months prior to the expiry date. Failure to provide such notice shall result in automatic renewal of the Agreement for a further period of 1 (One) year on the same commercial terms.

validation_warnings: AMBIGUITY: Clause 4.1 states payment is due on the '5th working day' while Schedule 2 states 'within 5 calendar days'. Recommend clarification from client.
NOTE: Escalation effective date is April 1 — invoices from January to March must use the prior-year WPI rate. This is a common error point during demos.
